title: Which market should they prioritise — and why does it matter now?
guidance: Reward the response that identifies the time-sensitive driver, not just the larger market. The decision window is the insight.

runner_input.instruction: Given the market data below, recommend which market this company should prioritise and explain why the timing matters.
runner_input.constraints: State one market. Explain the timing logic in 2–3 sentences. Do not enumerate pros and cons of each option.

source_blocks.text: SynthAnalytics is considering expanding from the UK into one of two markets: Germany or the US. It has £4m Series A runway (18 months). Its product is a B2B analytics platform for HR compliance data.

items: Germany: €2.1bn HR compliance market; Works Council legislation driving demand for structured HR data from firms >50 employees; no dominant analytics player in the segment; EU AI Act enforcement (2025–2026) adds a second compliance driver; sales cycle 4–6 months; requires German-language localisation (est. 3 months)

US: $18bn HR analytics market (total); highly competitive — 6 funded players with >£20m raised; enterprise buyers expect SOC2 Type II and US data residency (12+ months to achieve); ACV 3–4x higher than UK equivalently-sized deals; no time-bounded regulatory driver visible in next 18 months

rubric_notes: Germany is correct: two converging regulatory drivers (Works Council + EU AI Act), no dominant competitor, achievable in the 18-month runway window with localisation.
The US is structurally attractive but wrong on timing: 12+ months to achieve baseline enterprise requirements eats the runway, and there is no urgency trigger.
The insight is about the decision window, not the market size — the US is bigger but Germany is the only option that closes within the runway.
Penalise responses that recommend both or frame US as a later-phase option without explaining why it fails now.
